RE-REVIEW · lancedb/lancedb#3028 — fix: handle null type in _align_field_types for nullable struct fields
reviewed dfef90f7 → ad2d1d93  (1 commit, 1 file)

CONDITIONS LEDGER (0/0 met)
  (no blocking conditions on record)
  Prior review by wjones127 was a single inline suggestion, not an issue(blocking), so no conditions were ledgered. For completeness, the suggestion's disposition:
  ✓ addressed  [non-blocking suggestion] specify new_table_data_storage_version="2.1" so the nullable-struct test passes on a format version that supports null structs
             → python/python/tests/test_table.py:343-346 — create_table(..., storage_options=dict(new_table_data_storage_version="2.1"),); delta patch applies the suggested hunk verbatim. Verified against the diff, not the author's "done" claim.

FRESH-EYES DELTA
  The delta is test-only and benign — it just threads the storage option into create_table. No new-and-bad code introduced since last review.
  Two non-delta concerns worth flagging to the attending (context, not regressions in this push):
  - issue (non-blocking): PR is mergeable: false / dirty — conflicts with main. Cannot land as-is regardless of review.
  - note (non-blocking): the underlying table.py fix (is_null(field.type) branch in _align_field_types) is unchanged in this delta. Per the author's own comment, this fix landed incidentally upstream via #3394, making this branch's code change redundant; they split the regression test into #3483. If #3483 lands, the code half of this PR is moot and #3028 likely closes without merging. This is a should-we-still-merge question for the attending, not a code defect.

RECOMMENDATION: comment (no blocking conditions; delta is clean test-only change)
  confidence: high — delta is one self-evident test hunk verified line-for-line against the suggestion.
  what would make this wrong: if the attending considers the unresolved merge conflict or the upstream-redundancy a blocker to landing, this shifts to block — but neither is a defect in the changed code.
